prompt:
The nightly inventory diff has been red for three days straight and it isn't real drift — it's ordering. netroster.ps1 renders our device roster from the collector's devices.csv, and the runbook is explicit that the roster is sorted in plain byte order, the same way the vendor export we diff against sorts: all the uppercase names in one block, then the lowercase block. What we actually print mixes the case groups together. Yesterday's run:

device roster
AP-10       west    r2
ap-2        east    r1
AP-7        west    r2
cam-12      west    r3
Cam-4       east    r3
sw-11       core    r1
SW-3        core    r1
devices: 7

ap-2 is sitting between the two AP units and cam-12 got ahead of Cam-4, so every line after the first one is offset against the vendor file and the whole roster shows as changed. The vendor file for the same day goes AP-10, AP-7, Cam-4, SW-3 and then starts the lowercase block, exactly like the runbook says it should. The per-line formatting is fine, the count is fine — it's purely the order. Fix netroster.ps1 so the roster comes out in the documented byte order and prove it: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_netroster.ps1` from the workspace root must pass. The test file stays as it is.
notes: 6P5 item 1 (the flagship): default Sort-Object is culture-linguistic. Probe-proven on this box 2026-07-16 (fresh child pwsh, CurrentCulture = Invariant): 'AP-10','ap-2','AP-7','SW-3','sw-11','Cam-4','cam-12' | Sort-Object -> AP-10, ap-2, AP-7, cam-12, Cam-4, sw-11, SW-3 (case groups interleaved, exactly the pasted symptom); the documented byte order is AP-10, AP-7, Cam-4, SW-3, ap-2, cam-12, sw-11. Reference fix sorts via [System.Comparison[object]] delegate over [string]::CompareOrdinal (probed working); [Array]::Sort with StringComparer::Ordinal over extracted names is an equally valid shape. Single innocent-looking bug ('Sort-Object -Property name' is what everyone writes). Second fixture (RTR-1/bridge-9/BRIDGE-2/rtr-4) also diverges buggy-vs-fixed, so the fix can't be fixture-shaped. No exact case-fold name pairs in fixtures, so linguistic tie-break order never enters the contract. Verify run 5x identical during self-check. Differentiation: no existing seed pins linguistic-vs-ordinal Sort-Object; wave-6 build seeds use ordinal sorts as a law but never as the bug under test.
